clan_description: The imperial dynasty carries the weight of a glorious past and a crumbling present as they struggle to maintain the sanctity of the Peacock Throne. From their seat in Delhi they command the ultimate symbolic legitimacy in the subcontinent even as their actual territorial control shrinks to the surrounding districts. Their household troops are composed of the last remaining Mughal palace guards who are trained in the traditional arts of heavy cavalry and ceremonial defense. They represent a culture of high Persian refinement and central authority that is currently besieged by rising regional powers and internal court intrigues. This ruling clan operates on the prestige of their Timurid lineage which still compels even their enemies to seek imperial patents to legitimize their own conquests. Despite the lack of a treasury they maintain a complex court ritual that serves as the diplomatic language of the age. Their struggle is the ultimate tragedy of the era as they attempt to survive as sovereigns while essentially living as prisoners of their own protectors.

A clan of Persian origin that has become the premier military backbone of the imperial center through their expertise in modernizing the Mughal infantry. They are known for their fierce loyalty to the crown and their ability to raise disciplined battalions that can match the European trained sepoys of the coast. Their strength lies in their specialized musketeers and heavy cavalry who often serve as the vanguard in the emperors campaigns to reclaim lost provinces. In the current civil war they act as the primary defense force against the encroaching Marathas and rebellious regional governors. This group represents the influx of Shia military talent that sought to revitalize the dying empire through merit and rigorous drill. Unlike the older nobility they embrace modern flintlock technology and bayonet tactics to compensate for the decline in traditional heavy cavalry numbers. Their survival is tied directly to the Emperors personal fortune and they are often the only force preventing the total sack of the capital by marauding tribal bands.

A powerful confederacy of Afghan settlers who have carved out a significant territory in the fertile lands north of the capital known as Rohilkhand. They are often viewed with suspicion by the imperial court due to their tendency to align with the invading Durranis to protect their local autonomy. Their military power is based on rugged mountain infantry and light skirmishers who are masters of the long range matchlock. They represent the volatile frontier element of the empire that often dictates who holds power in the streets of Delhi through sheer martial force. Their social structure is fiercely tribal and egalitarian compared to the rigid Mughal hierarchy which allows them to mobilize an entire population of fighting men during times of crisis. They utilize the terrain of the foothills to launch devastating ambushes making any attempt to tax their lands a suicide mission for imperial collectors. During the civil war they serve as the kingmakers of the north often switching sides to ensure that no single power can ever fully dominate their borders.

A clan of Pathans settled in the Farrukhabad region who serve as a critical military buffer between the imperial heartland and the rising powers of the south. They are renowned for their bravery and their specialized knowledge of artillery manufacturing which makes them a valuable ally for any claimant to the throne. Their territory acts as a strategic gateway and they have spent generations defending this corridor against both the Marathas and the Nawabs of Awadh. They maintain a distinct cultural identity that prizes individual martial skill and the maintenance of a professional standing army of seasoned veterans. Their role in the civil war is one of survival through strategic alliance often providing the heavy muscle for imperial coalitions in exchange for land rights. They are the quintessential border lords of the 18th century possessing a military discipline that is respected even by their most bitter enemies.

The powerful dynasty of the Nawabs of Awadh who serve as the hereditary Grand Viziers of the empire and possess the most formidable field army in the north. They govern a vast and fertile territory that has become the new economic center of the dying empire providing the riches needed to sustain a massive military machine. Their troops are a sophisticated blend of traditional heavy cavalry and massive battalions of Purbiya infantry who are famous for their height and discipline. This clan represents the peak of the successor state model where the wealth of a province is used to maintain the facade of imperial service while building a sovereign power base. Their intervention in the civil war is often the deciding factor in any major campaign as they bring with them the finest artillery train in the subcontinent. They are the ultimate pragmatists using their position as Viziers to claim legitimacy while their real power flows from their independent treasury and bayonets.

Descendants of former Grand Viziers who represent the old guard of the Turani nobility and the traditional Central Asian factions within the court. They maintain vast estates and a network of clients that dates back to the reign of Aurangzeb making them a conservative force in a rapidly changing world. Their military retinues are composed of elite heavy cavalry who still adhere to the ancient tactics of shock charges and archery. This group struggles to adapt to the new age of gunpowder and often finds themselves at odds with the newer Persian and Afghan adventurers rising in the capital. They act as the keepers of the old imperial traditions and provide a sense of continuity for the bureaucracy during the chaotic shifts of the civil war. Their influence is largely political and social using their high lineage to broker deals between the warring factions in the imperial palace.

An ancient and celebrated clan of Indian Muslims who have traditionally held the ancestral right to lead the imperial vanguard in every major battle since the empire's inception. They are renowned for their fanatical bravery and a martial code that forbids them from retreating from the field of battle. Their warriors are mostly heavy infantry and cavalry who specialize in close quarters combat with the sword and shield. In the context of the civil war they represent the native military elite who feel increasingly sidelined by the rise of foreign mercenaries and modern artillery. They remain fiercely loyal to the concept of the empire if not always the person of the emperor serving as the symbolic guardians of the Indian Muslim warrior tradition. Their fortresses are scattered across the Doab region providing a network of defensive points that any invader must overcome to reach the capital.

The clan traditionally entrusted with the command of the imperial artillery corps and the defense of the strategic red stone battlements of the capital. They are masters of the technical aspects of 18th century warfare including the casting of massive bronze cannons and the management of large gunpowder magazines. Their soldiers are specialized technicians and gunners who form the backbone of any defensive siege operation conducted by the emperor. During the civil war they are the last line of defense for the House of Timur ensuring that the Red Fort remains an impregnable bastion against rebel forces. They possess a unique social status as the keepers of the empires most advanced and destructive weapons making their support vital for any claimant to the throne. Their expertise is a closely guarded secret passed down through generations of master gunners and siege engineers.

A clan of Central Asian warriors who have served as the elite palace guards and the personal heavy shock cavalry of the Timurids for several centuries. They are recognized by their distinct armor and their mastery of the composite bow and heavy lance which they use with devastating effect in open field battles. Unlike the local levies they are entirely dependent on the Emperors personal patronage which ensures their unwavering loyalty during palace coups. They represent the fading connection between the Mughal court and its nomadic roots in the steppes of Central Asia. In the current conflict they serve as the personal retinue of the emperor often being the only force he can truly trust when the provincial governors turn against him. Their presence in the capital is a reminder of the empires origins and they remain the most feared individual combatants in the streets of Delhi.

The ruling royal house of the Afghan empire which successfully unified the disparate tribes of the Hindu Kush into a singular conquering force. They are the architects of the great southward invasions and view the crumbling Mughal lands as their rightful spoils of war. Their military doctrine centers on the devastating charge of the heavy armored cavalry supported by a vast network of tribal levies. As the masters of the north they seek to impose a new order on the plains of India through a mix of religious fervor and overwhelming military might. This ruling clan has mastered the art of tribal diplomacy ensuring that the fierce independence of the Afghans is channeled into foreign conquest rather than internal feud. Their reign is marked by a series of high stakes invasions that have shattered the power of the Mughals and the Marathas alike. They represent a new and hungry imperial power that seeks to reclaim the legacy of the old Delhi Sultanates.

A prestigious and numerous clan that provides the empire with its most capable generals and regional governors while maintaining a deep rivalry with the ruling house. They are known for their immense political influence and their ability to mobilize thousands of veteran warriors from the Afghan heartland at a moments notice. Their troops are characterized by their extreme mobility and their proficiency with the jezail musket which allows them to dominate the mountain passes. Within the confederacy they serve as a critical power block that ensures the empires expansion remains focused on the rich lands of the Punjab. This clan acts as the administrators of the newly conquered territories often bringing a harsh but effective tribal law to the chaotic borderlands. Their loyalty is the glue that holds the Afghan military machine together but it is a loyalty that must be constantly bought with the spoils of war. They are the rising stars of the Afghan world preparing for a future where they might one day claim the supreme leadership for themselves.

The most prestigious branch of the Durranis providing the empire with its highest ministers and the civil administrators who manage the transition from conquest to governance. They are recognized for their refined culture and their ability to navigate the complex tribal politics of Kabul and Kandahar. Their military contribution is found in the elite household cavalry units that serve as the emperors personal guard during his Indian campaigns. They represent the intellectual and political elite of the Afghan state seeking to build a permanent empire rather than a mere raiding confederacy. During the civil war in the south they act as the primary diplomats who negotiate treaties with the various Indian powers. Their goal is to create a stable northern frontier that can serve as a permanent base for Afghan influence in the subcontinent. They are the strategic brains behind the raw brawn of the Afghan tribal hordes.

A clan famous for providing the elite cavalry commanders who spearheaded the most daring invasions of the Indian plains during the mid century. They are specialists in the use of the camel mounted swivel gun which provides a mobile and terrifying source of firepower on the battlefield. Their warriors are raised from birth to be masters of the saddle and are known for their ability to cover vast distances with minimal supplies. This clan represents the peak of Afghan tactical mobility capable of outmaneuvering the slow moving Mughal columns with ease. During the current conflict they serve as the fast moving vanguard that strikes at enemy supply lines and prevents the consolidation of rebel forces. Their reputation for ferocity is such that many garrisons surrender at the mere sight of their banners on the horizon. They are the primary agents of shock and awe in the Durrani military machine.

Mountain dwelling warriors known for their fierce independence and their absolute expertise in guerrilla warfare within the narrow passes of the frontier. They are the masters of the heights often engaging in long range marksmanship that can decimate an invading army before it even sees an enemy. Their social structure is decentralized with power resting in the hands of local chieftains who prize honor and blood vengeance above all else. They serve as the guardians of the empires western mountain borders ensuring that no threat can emerge from the desert reaches. In the context of the Indian campaigns they are used as specialized mountain infantry who excel in siege operations and rocky terrain. Their loyalty is often temporary driven by the promise of plunder and the defense of their ancestral valleys. They represent the raw and untamable spirit of the Afghan highlands that has broken many empires before them.

A formerly dominant tribe that remains a powerful and sometimes rebellious force within the Afghan confederacy possessing a deep memory of their own past kings. They are known for their incredible physical resilience and a military tradition that emphasizes massive infantry charges backed by tribal musketry. Their relationship with the ruling house is one of tense cooperation as they are too powerful to ignore but too independent to fully control. They provide the empire with some of its most seasoned veterans who have fought in every theater from the Persian border to the banks of the Yamuna. Their struggle is one of reclaiming their lost prestige within the Afghan hierarchy often leading the charge in the most dangerous sectors of the front. They represent a dormant power that could either secure the empires future or tear it apart from within if their grievances are not addressed.

Masters of the Khyber Pass who demand tribute from every army that moves between Kabul and India and whose control over the trade routes is absolute. They are specialists in defensive warfare and ambush tactics using the jagged terrain of the passes to hold off much larger forces with ease. Their warriors are world renowned for their marksmanship and their ability to craft their own high quality firearms in hidden mountain workshops. They act as the gatekeepers of the empire ensuring that the vital link between the Afghan heartland and the Indian provinces remains secure. Their role in the civil war is that of a necessary ally for the Durranis as no invasion can succeed without their cooperation and guidance through the passes. They are fiercely proud and independent often operating as a state within a state that answers to no one but their own tribal councils.

A large and influential tribe settled in the Rohilkhand and Peshawar regions specializing in flintlock musketry and disciplined tribal warfare. They have a long history of migration and conquest often serving as the primary settlers in newly won territories in northern India. Their military power is unique in its blend of traditional Afghan courage and the adoption of modern infantry formations. This clan represents the bridge between the Afghan mountains and the Indian plains often becoming the local lords of the lands they help conquer. During the imperial collapse they have built a powerful sub state in northern India that serves as a permanent base for Afghan interests. Their leaders are as much politicians as they are warriors skillfully navigating the treacherous waters of the Mughal court. They are the most settled and organized of the Afghan tribes in the subcontinent.

A loyalist clan that manages the western frontiers and provides the empire with seasoned frontier scouts and light cavalry. They are experts in the geography of the arid plains and the tracking of enemy movements over vast distances making them the eyes and ears of the emperor. Their warriors are known for their endurance and their ability to survive in the most inhospitable environments while maintaining peak combat readiness. They provide the vital intelligence that allows the Afghan commanders to strike with precision against the fragmented Mughal forces. In the civil war they serve as the internal security force that prevents tribal uprisings and secures the empires supply lines. Their loyalty is a cornerstone of the Sadozai administration providing a reliable and steady force in an otherwise volatile tribal landscape. They are the silent professionals of the Afghan military who ensure that the grand strategy of the emperor is successfully executed on the ground.

The ruling house of the Nawabs of Bengal who preside over the most industrially productive and fertile province of the entire subcontinent from their capital in Murshidabad. They have transitioned from being imperial governors to independent sovereigns who control the vast wealth generated by the global silk and textile trade. Their military strength is derived from a massive revenue base that allows them to hire large numbers of professional mercenaries and maintain a sprawling riverine navy. They are currently caught in a desperate struggle to maintain their autonomy while facing internal conspiracies and the growing pressure of foreign commercial interests. This ruling clan navigates a world of high stakes diplomacy where the betrayal of a general can be as deadly as an invading army. Their court is a center of immense luxury and intrigue where the fate of the province is often decided behind closed doors. They represent the ultimate prize for any conqueror as their lands provide the silver that fuels the wars of the entire subcontinent.

A legendary clan of merchant bankers whose financial empire is so vast that they effectively dictate the economic policies of the entire province. They do not field traditional armies but instead use their immense wealth to fund the campaigns of their allies and influence the succession of the nawabi throne. Their power is felt in every market and treasury across the land making them indispensable to anyone who wishes to rule Bengal. They represent the transition of the province into a modern commercial state where gold often carries more weight than the sword. Their influence extends to the global stage as they are the primary financiers for the various European East India Companies operating in the delta. In the current civil war they act as the ultimate power brokers deciding which claimant to the nawabi throne is most beneficial to the stability of trade. Their security is maintained by a private army of seasoned mercenaries who guard their massive vaults and trade caravans.

High ranking administrators who oversee the revenue and judicial systems of the vast eastern delta and ensure the steady flow of wealth to the capital. They are masters of the complex land management systems of Bengal and possess a deep knowledge of the local customs and laws. Their influence is built on their indispensable bureaucratic expertise which keeps the state functioning even during times of political chaos. They represent the administrative class that bridges the gap between the nawabi court and the agrarian population of the countryside. During the imperial decline they have taken on a more active role in provincial defense organizing local militias to protect the trade routes. Their loyalty is to the stability of the province and they are often the first to negotiate with foreign powers to ensure the continuity of the legal system. They are the silent architects of the Bengal state who ensure that the machinery of government never stops turning.

A rival branch of the nawabi family based in the northern district of Purnea who frequently contest the throne in Murshidabad and maintain a separate military establishment. They represent the internal dynastic friction that has plagued the province for generations often seeking outside support to further their claims. Their military is composed of hardened border troops who are accustomed to fighting off incursions from the Himalayan foothills. This clan serves as a constant threat to the central authority of the Nawab forcing him to divert resources to the north that could be used elsewhere. Their territory is a bastion of traditionalist sentiment where the old ways of the Mughal subahdars are still strictly observed. During the civil war they act as a wild card often joining forces with the Marathas or the Mughals to destabilize the current ruler of Murshidabad. They are the primary internal challengers to the status quo in the delta.

A clan of reformers and nationalists who seek to modernize the provincial army and remove the stifling influence of foreign trading companies from the local economy. They are known for their attempts to shift the capital away from the coastal intrigues and build a new professionalized state in the interior. Their military doctrine emphasizes the creation of a standing army drilled in modern tactics and equipped with locally manufactured high quality firearms. This group represents the last major effort by the Bengali elite to reassert total sovereignty over their lands before the onset of colonial rule. Their struggle is one of intense modernization and political purification aiming to create a state that can compete on equal terms with any global power. They are the visionary faction within the Bengal nobility often finding themselves isolated by the very bankers and merchants they seek to control.

The most powerful Hindu landowning clan in Bengal maintaining their own private armies and vast estates that cover thousands of square miles. They act as the primary intermediaries between the Nawab and the rural peasantry ensuring the collection of revenue and the maintenance of order in the heartland. Their military force is composed of traditional Barkandazes and local levies who are expert in the defense of the many small forts that dot the landscape. This clan represents the enduring power of the indigenous landed aristocracy who have survived every change of dynasty in the delta. During the civil war they serve as the stabilizing force of the interior often providing the Nawab with the large numbers of infantry needed for his campaigns. Their loyalty is highly prized as their defection could lead to the total economic collapse of the provincial government. They are the true masters of the soil in Bengal.

A military lineage that has defended the province western borders against the devastating Maratha invasions for several decades with incredible tenacity. They are specialists in defensive warfare and the construction of massive earthworks designed to slow down the fast moving enemy cavalry. Their warriors are seasoned veterans of some of the most brutal fighting in the 18th century and are known for their high morale and tactical discipline. This clan represents the shield of Bengal providing the safety necessary for the trade and agriculture of the delta to flourish. During the current conflict they serve as the vanguards of the provincial army always stationed at the most dangerous frontiers. Their reputation for resilience has made them the most respected military faction in the eyes of the Nawab. They are the hardened veterans who have seen the worst of the wars and emerged as the defenders of the state.

An influential noble family based in the east overseeing the global trade of fine silks and muslins and governing the ancient city of Dacca. They represent the cultural and commercial sophistication of the eastern provinces which provide a significant portion of the states total revenue. Their military contribution is focused on the protection of the vast river networks ensuring that the trade fleets are safe from both pirates and rival powers. This clan maintains a court that rivals Murshidabad in its patronage of the arts and its connection to the global Islamic world. During the civil war they act as the eastern anchor of the state providing a secure base of operations and a steady supply of resources. Their influence is built on their control over the specialized artisans and merchants who produce the legendary textiles of Bengal. They are the guardians of the provinces most valuable and delicate treasures.

A clan of wealthy intermediaries and power brokers who navigate the dangerous and murky politics between the Nawab and the growing influence of European powers. They are experts in the art of the secret deal using their vast networks of informants and their deep pockets to influence the course of events. Their power is informal but absolute as no major political or economic move can be made in the province without their involvement. They represent the transition of the Bengali elite into a class of global players who understand the shifting tides of the 18th century. In the civil war they serve as the primary negotiators often moving between the camps of rival claimants to find a peaceful and profitable resolution. Their loyalty is often questioned but their utility is so high that every Nawab finds them indispensable. They are the grand masters of the shadow politics that define the fate of the delta.

The sovereign dynasty that governs the vast Deccan plateau by blending the refined administrative traditions of the Mughals with local southern power structures. They manage a wealthy and stable state that acts as the primary guardian of Islamic culture in the south while navigating a sea of enemies. Their military relies on a sophisticated mix of traditional heavy cavalry and modernizing infantry battalions supported by a professional bureaucracy. In the ongoing imperial collapse they seek to preserve their independence while playing the various warring factions against each other to maintain the balance of power. This ruling clan has built a courtly culture in the heart of the south that attracts poets scholars and warriors from across the Islamic world. Their authority is based on a delicate balance of providing stability to the local Hindu chieftains while maintaining a strong Muslim military elite. They are the enduring successors of the Mughal dream in the Deccan defending their borders with a mix of high diplomacy and iron force.

An elite group of nobles who are bound by oath to the personal service of the ruler and hold the most prestigious military commissions in the state. They are entrusted with the security of the capital and the command of the household troops which are considered the finest in the Deccan. Their influence extends beyond the battlefield into the highest levels of courtly politics where they act as the protectors of the dynasty. They represent the ultimate expression of the feudal loyalty that keeps the state united against the twin threats of the Marathas and Mysore. This clan is unique in their privilege of maintaining their own private standing armies which are integrated into the national defense during times of war. Their family history is one of total devotion to the Nizam often serving as the foster brothers and personal companions to the royal princes. They are the heavy hand of the state and the most trusted defenders of the Asaf Jahi throne.

A family of elite diplomats and statesmen who provide the administrative brilliance and the visionary leadership required to keep the diverse Deccan provinces united. They are masters of the art of the treaty often securing the kingdoms borders through skillful negotiation with the British French and Marathas. Their power is rooted in their indispensable knowledge of international law and their ability to manage the kingdoms complex financial systems. This clan represents the intellectual engine of Hyderabad ensuring that the state remains relevant and sovereign in a rapidly changing world. During the civil war they act as the primary strategists who decide which alliances will best serve the long term survival of the Nizam. Their influence is felt in every department of the government from the collection of taxes to the modernization of the legal code. They are the sophisticated statesmen who ensure that the sword is always guided by a clear and clever mind.

A military clan that holds the strategic fortress of Kalyani and provides the heavy shock cavalry that serves as the primary offensive weapon of the Nizam. They are specialists in the siege of hill forts and the conduct of large scale cavalry maneuvers across the rocky terrain of the plateau. Their warriors are famous for their physical prowess and their ability to execute complex battle plans under the most intense pressure. This clan represents the traditional military elite of the Deccan who have remained loyal to the state through centuries of conflict. In the current civil war they serve as the vanguards of the army always stationed at the most contested borders to repel Maratha incursions. Their leadership is defined by a deep sense of honor and a commitment to the martial traditions of the Deccani sultanates. They are the iron shield of Hyderabad holding the line against the encroaching chaos.

The Nawabs of the Carnatic who manage the southern coastal reaches of the state and provide the primary link between the interior and the maritime trade routes. They are caught in a difficult position between the central authority of the Nizam and the rising power of Mysore and the British. Their military is composed of specialized coastal infantry and a small but effective navy that protects the southern ports from piracy. This clan represents the southern frontier of the Deccani world often serving as the primary theater for the wars against foreign colonial interests. During the civil war they act as a vital source of revenue and intelligence for the Nizam although their loyalty is often tested by their proximity to other powers. They are the coastal guardians of the south navigating a world where the sea is just as dangerous as the land.

Descendants of African military elites who have served the Deccani sultanates for many centuries and remain famous for their incredible physical strength and absolute loyalty. They are the preferred choice for elite palace guards and the personal bodyguards of the high nobility due to their reputation for never betraying a master. Their military specialty is in close quarters combat and the defense of urban environments where their resilience makes them a nightmare for attackers. This clan represents a unique and enduring part of the Deccani social fabric proving that merit and martial skill can transcend geographic origins. In the civil war they are the last line of defense for the Nizam often being the only force that remains at the sovereigns side during times of total collapse. Their presence in the court is a testament to the diverse and inclusive nature of the southern Islamic states. They are the unbreakable warriors of the Deccan.

A powerful diplomatic and administrative faction that specializes in negotiating the complex and shifting alliances between the state and the European powers. They are the masters of the game of thrones using their deep understanding of French and British interests to preserve the sovereignty of Hyderabad. Their influence is built on their control over the information networks and the secret correspondence of the state. This clan represents the modernizing wing of the nobility who recognize that the future of the Deccan depends on the successful management of foreign relations. During the imperial decline they serve as the primary architects of the subsidiary alliances that have provided the state with modern military technology. Their goal is to ensure that Hyderabad survives as a sovereign power regardless of who eventually wins the war in the north. They are the clever pragmatists who protect the state through the power of the written word.

A group of northern aristocrats who manage the fertile cotton lands of the Berar province and act as a critical buffer between the Nizam and the Maratha heartland. They possess a dual identity often having to balance their loyalty to the Hyderabad state with the cultural and linguistic ties they share with the Maratha neighbors. Their military force is composed of local levies and light cavalry who are experts in the defense of the northern plateau passes. This clan represents the northern frontier lords who are the first to feel the impact of any southern movement by the Maratha confederacy. In the civil war they serve as the primary scouts and border guards providing the Nizam with early warning of any impending invasion. Their position is one of constant vigilance and they have become masters of the art of frontier defense. They are the northern sentinels of the Deccan state.

A prestigious noble house known for their magnificent palaces and their patronage of the arts as well as their command of highly disciplined household troops. They represent the peak of the cultural and social elite in Hyderabad providing the state with its most refined courtiers and military officers. Their military retinues are small but incredibly well equipped often serving as the elite heavy cavalry in the Nizams field army. This clan is dedicated to the preservation of the Persianate culture of the south and the maintenance of the high standards of Deccani chivalry. During the civil war they provide the state with its most reliable and well trained officers who are capable of leading diverse forces with confidence. Their loyalty is to the concept of the civilized state and they are the primary opponents of any form of tribal or populist uprising. They are the refined protectors of the Deccani social order.

A rising power among the Sikh misls that has distinguished itself through its relentless resistance against both Mughal governors and Afghan invaders. Based in the central Punjab they have built a reputation for tactical brilliance and an unyielding commitment to the sovereignty of the Khalsa. Their warriors are famous for their skill in horse archery and their ability to disappear into the jungles only to strike back with renewed vigor. They are the primary architects of the local defense system that has turned the Punjab into a graveyard for foreign ambitions. This ruling clan has mastered the art of guerrilla warfare using the fertile plains of the five rivers to outmaneuver the slow moving imperial armies. Their rise is fueled by a sense of divine mission and a commitment to the egalitarian principles of their faith which allows them to recruit the finest fighting men from across the region. They are the destined unifiers of the Sikh people preparing the ground for a future empire that will stretch from the Khyber to the Yamuna.

The clan led by the supreme commander of the Dal Khalsa who is respected by all Sikh misls as a unifying religious and military leader of unparalleled integrity. They are the glue that holds the various independent Sikh factions together providing a centralized command structure during the most dangerous invasions. Their warriors are elite horsemen who specialize in the coordination of large scale cavalry maneuvers and the defense of the sacred community. This clan represents the selfless and spiritual core of the Sikh resistance prioritizing the survival of the faith over any individual territorial gain. In the context of the civil war they act as the ultimate arbiters between the rival misls ensuring that the internal conflicts never jeopardize the security of the Punjab. Their leadership is defined by a deep humility and a martial prowess that has been forged in decades of constant warfare against the Afghans. They are the noble heart of the Sikh confederacy.

Masters of fortification and artillery who rebuilt the sacred fort of Ram Rauni and have turned the northern hills into an impregnable bastion for the Sikh people. They are renowned for their technical skills in engineering and the manufacturing of high quality weaponry which they provide to the entire confederacy. Their warriors are expert in the defense of forts and the conduct of protracted sieges making them the primary engineers of the Sikh military. This clan represents the industrial and technical wing of the Khalsa proving that the Sikh resistance is as much about modern technology as it is about traditional bravery. During the imperial collapse they have carved out a secure territory in the foothills providing a safe haven for the community during times of invasion. Their independence is fiercely guarded and they often act as a separate power block within the confederacy due to their unique technical advantages. They are the builders and the smiths of the Sikh revolution.

The clan that first organized the Sikhs into a formal military confederacy to fight the Mughals and has since served as the traditional guardians of the Khalsa's military code. They are known for their extreme discipline and their commitment to the early traditions of the Sikh warrior saints. Their military force is composed of highly dedicated veterans who specialize in the training of new recruits and the maintenance of the community's morale. This clan represents the historical memory of the Sikh resistance reminding the newer generations of the sacrifices made by the early gurus and their followers. In the current conflict they serve as the moral compass of the confederacy often intervening to stop the excesses of the more ambitious misl leaders. Their influence is based on their long history of service and their reputation for absolute incorruptibility in the face of political temptation. They are the venerable elders of the Sikh military order.

A highly mobile clan that specializes in lightning raids and the guarding of the strategic northern river crossings against any sudden movement from the Afghan mountains. They are masters of the hit and run tactic using their superior knowledge of the local geography to harass enemy supply lines with devastating efficiency. Their warriors are light cavalry who pride themselves on their speed and their ability to live off the land for months at a time. This clan represents the mobile eyes and ears of the Sikh confederacy providing early warning of any impending threat to the Punjabi heartland. During the civil war they serve as the primary scouts and skirmishers who prevent the consolidation of enemy forces on the borders. Their role is one of constant movement and they are often the first to strike and the last to leave the field of battle. They are the swift and silent rangers of the Sikh world.

A clan that controls the fertile lands between Lahore and Multan focusing on heavy cavalry and the agrarian defense of the southern Punjabi plains. They are unique among the misls for their emphasis on agricultural self sufficiency and the building of large fortified villages that can withstand prolonged raids. Their warriors are resilient landowning soldiers who fight to protect their crops and their families from the constant incursions of the fading Mughal governors. This clan represents the settled and prosperous side of the Sikh community proving that the Khalsa can govern and farm as well as they can fight. In the current conflict they act as the southern shield of the confederacy preventing any resurgence of imperial power from the direction of Multan. Their strength is rooted in their deep connection to the soil and their ability to sustain a long term war of attrition. They are the sturdy and reliable defenders of the Sikh breadbasket.

A powerful misl that dominates the foothills of the Himalayas and manages the lucrative trade routes that lead to the mountains of Kashmir and beyond. They are known for their wealth and their ability to recruit mountain warriors into the Sikh ranks adding a diverse range of tactical options to the confederacy. Their military force is a mix of traditional cavalry and specialized mountain infantry who excel in high altitude combat and narrow pass defense. This clan represents the northern expansion of the Sikh state integrating the diverse cultures of the hills into the egalitarian fold of the Khalsa. During the imperial decline they have taken over the role of the primary protectors of the northern merchants ensuring that the flow of goods is never interrupted by the chaos of the plains. Their influence is built on their control over the vital mountain passes and their diplomatic skill in dealing with the various hill rajas. They are the northern merchant kings of the Sikh world.

The rulers of Patiala who often act as the primary diplomats between the Sikh confederacy and the crumbling Mughal center as well as the invading Afghan powers. They are known for their sophisticated political maneuvering using their strategic location to protect the interests of the Sikh community through negotiation as much as through force. Their military is composed of a well organized standing army that bridges the gap between the tribal misls and the traditional state structures. This clan represents the diplomatic and administrative talent of the Sikh people seeking to build a lasting political entity that can be recognized by the surrounding powers. In the context of the civil war they serve as the buffer state that prevents a total and direct collision between the Afghans and the interior of the subcontinent. Their role is one of immense complexity as they must balance the demands of the Khalsa with the harsh realities of international politics. They are the clever and resilient statesmen of the Sikh revolution.

A highly aggressive clan that has expanded the Sikh influence into the territories surrounding Delhi and the upper reaches of the Doab. They are known for their bold raids into the heart of the Mughal capital and their ability to extract tribute from the imperial administrators. Their warriors are seasoned cavalrymen who specialize in deep penetration missions and the capturing of enemy supply trains. This clan represents the expansionist and vengeful spirit of the Sikh people seeking to punish the Mughal center for its history of persecution. During the civil war they serve as the primary offensive vanguard that keeps the imperial court in a state of constant fear. Their leadership is defined by a focus on military conquest and the permanent displacement of the old Mughal nobility from the northern plains. They are the daring and relentless warriors of the Sikh frontier.

A revolutionary military dynasty that has surged to power by displacing the old order and implementing radical administrative and military reforms. They have transformed a small southern kingdom into a centralized powerhouse that utilizes modern technology like iron cased rockets to shatter traditional formations. Their infantry is rigorously trained in European tactics making them one of the few indigenous forces capable of standing toe to toe with colonial armies. They are defined by their fierce independence and their ambition to dominate the entire southern peninsula through meritocracy and innovation. This ruling clan has abolished the feudal land systems in favor of a direct tax model allowing them to fund a professional standing army that is loyal only to the state. Their court is a center of scientific inquiry and global diplomacy often seeking alliances as far away as France and the Ottoman Empire. They represent a new breed of Indian statehood that rejects the decadence of the past in favor of a disciplined and modernized future.

The ancient Hindu royal family who remain as the titular heads of the state providing the essential continuity and religious legitimacy that the people require. While the actual power has shifted to the military reformers they still maintain a prestigious court and represent the long history of the Mysore region. Their influence is felt in the cultural and religious spheres where they act as the primary patrons of the local arts and temples. This clan represents the traditional soul of the kingdom ensuring that the rapid changes of the modernizers do not alienate the traditional population. In the context of the civil war they provide the symbolic center around which the diverse people of the south can unite during times of external threat. Their presence is a reminder of the kingdoms roots and they serve as the essential guardians of the local identity. They are the venerable and respected elders of the southern world.

A clan of powerful hereditary ministers who once ruled the state as regents before being sidelined by the rise of the new military elite. They represent the old aristocratic order of Mysore and maintain significant influence in the rural districts through their ancient land holdings. Their military retinues are composed of traditional local warriors who are loyal to the family name and their long history of service to the throne. This clan exists in a state of tense coexistence with the new reformers often acting as the primary voice for the traditionalist nobility in the court. During the current conflict they serve as the regional governors who ensure that the countryside remains stable while the main armies are away on campaign. Their struggle is one of maintaining their relevance in a world that is increasingly dominated by professional soldiers and modern technology. They are the proud remnants of the ancient Mysorean aristocracy.

An influential branch of the Mysore nobility that controls the strategic hill forts surrounding the capital and has a long history of military service to the state. They are specialists in the defense of high places and the conduct of protracted sieges using the rugged terrain of the plateau to their advantage. Their warriors are hardened mountain infantry who are experts in the use of traditional weapons and local knowledge. This clan represents the traditional defensive backbone of the kingdom providing the safe havens that allow the central government to function during times of invasion. In the civil war they serve as the primary guardians of the heartland ensuring that the capital remains secure against any sudden raid. Their loyalty is based on their deep connection to the land and their historical role as the protectors of the Mysorean people. They are the rock upon which the kingdoms defense is built.

A clan of local administrators and commanders who manage the eastern fertile valleys of the Kaveri river and provide the state with its primary source of food and tax revenue. They are known for their expertise in irrigation and agrarian management ensuring that the kingdoms resources are used to their fullest potential. Their military force is composed of well armed local levies who are trained to defend the vital river crossings and agricultural centers. This clan represents the agrarian strength of Mysore proving that a modern military machine is only as strong as its economic base. During the imperial decline they have taken over the role of the primary logistical officers for the army ensuring that the troops are always well supplied and fed. Their influence is built on their indispensable role in the kingdoms economy making them one of the most stable and reliable factions in the south. They are the hardworking and efficient providers of the Mysorean state.

The naval commanders and merchant lords of the coast who provide the kingdom with vital access to global trade networks and modern firearms from overseas. They are specialists in maritime warfare and the defense of the lush coastal ports against both European fleets and local pirates. Their warriors are expert sailors and marines who bring a unique amphibious dimension to the kingdoms military capabilities. This clan represents the outward looking and commercial side of Mysore seeking to turn the kingdom into a major naval power in the Indian Ocean. In the context of the civil war they serve as the primary procurement officers for the state using their trade connections to acquire the latest military technology. Their influence is rooted in their control over the spice trade and their ability to navigate the complex world of international commerce. They are the daring and wealthy sea lords of the southern coast.

A fierce highland clan that frequently rebels against central authority from their forested mountain strongholds and maintains a unique cultural and martial identity. They are renowned for their incredible marksmanship and their ability to conduct devastating guerrilla raids through the dense jungles of the Western Ghats. Their warriors are individualistic and brave fighting with a fanatical devotion to their local gods and their ancestral lands. This clan represents the untamable frontier of the south providing a constant challenge to any power that seeks to consolidate the entire region. During the civil war they act as a volatile and unpredictable force often siding with whoever can guarantee their independence and protect their sacred forests. Their role is one of fierce local resistance and they have become legendary for their ability to defeat much larger forces in the deep woods. They are the wild and free warriors of the southern mountains.

Former Mughal governors who now serve as the northern guardians and administrators of the kingdoms expanded territories in the central Deccan. They bring with them the refined administrative and military traditions of the north adding a layer of professional expertise to the Mysorean government. Their military force is a mix of traditional Deccani cavalry and locally recruited infantry who are trained in the latest tactical drills. This clan represents the successful integration of the old imperial elite into the new and dynamic Mysorean state. In the current conflict they serve as the primary border guards in the north preventing any southern expansion by the Marathas or the Nizam. Their influence is built on their ability to manage a diverse and sometimes restless population providing the stability needed for the kingdoms northern provinces to flourish. They are the professional and experienced administrators of the southern expansion.

A clan of southern chieftains who guard the strategic passes into the lush Kerala plains and provide the kingdom with elite mountain infantry and scouts. They are experts in the geography of the southern gaps and the conduct of warfare in high humidity and dense vegetation. Their warriors are known for their endurance and their mastery of traditional martial arts making them formidable in close quarters combat. This clan represents the southern reach of the Mysorean state securing the vital routes that connect the plateau with the rich coastal lands of the south. During the civil war they serve as the primary guardians of the southern frontier ensuring that no threat can emerge from the direction of the Travancore state. Their loyalty is highly prized as their control over the passes makes them the gatekeepers of the southern trade. They are the resilient and watchful sentinels of the southern gaps.

The premier diplomatic and political house of Rajasthan which has long served as the primary bridge between the Rajput clans and the imperial center. From their magnificent city of Jaipur they coordinate the defense of the union and manage the complex network of alliances that keep the desert states sovereign. Their military is a mix of traditional clan levies and professional musketry battalions that reflect their adaptability to the changing face of warfare. They represent the sophisticated face of Rajput power that uses both the sword and the pen to preserve their ancestral lands. This ruling clan has built a legacy of architectural and scientific brilliance turning their capital into a center of learning that attracts talent from across the subcontinent. Their leadership is defined by a pragmatic approach to power seeking to preserve the Rajput way of life through strategic cooperation rather than futile resistance. They are the grand architects of the Rajput union navigating the collapse of the Mughals with grace and foresight.

The proud and fierce lords of Jodhpur who are renowned for their legendary courage and their mastery of the desert terrain. They are famous for their tradition of the saffron robe where warriors swear to fight to the death rather than accept the dishonor of defeat. Their cavalry is considered among the finest in the world capable of executing devastating charges across the shifting sands of the Marwar. They are the most stubborn opponents of any centralizing force and remain the primary defenders of the western frontiers of the union. This clan maintains a martial culture that is deeply rooted in the ancient codes of the Kshatriya where every man is a soldier and every woman a guardian of honor. During the current civil war they serve as the primary offensive force of the union launching deep raids to reclaim ancestral lands lost to the Mughals. Their spirit is as unyielding as the desert sun and they remain the heart of the Rajput resistance.

The most ancient and honored house among all Rajputs serving as the symbolic spiritual center of the resistance and the keepers of the sacred flame of Chittor. They are known for their uncompromising stance on sovereignty and their history of enduring the most brutal sieges in Indian history rather than bowing to a foreign emperor. Their warriors are traditionalists who pride themselves on their skill with the sword and the spear fighting with a sense of divine duty. This clan represents the unyielding soul of the Rajput people providing a moral anchor for the other clans during times of crisis. In the context of the civil war they act as the ultimate arbiters of honor ensuring that the union remains true to its ancestral traditions. Their fortresses are the holiest sites in Rajasthan and their banners are the rallying point for every warrior who dreams of a free Rajputana. They are the eternal and sacred guardians of the Rajput legacy.

A clan known for their suicidal bravery and their absolute refusal to ever turn their backs on an enemy regardless of the odds they face in battle. They are the specialists of the vanguard often being the first to charge and the last to retreat during the most intense clashes of the 18th century. Their military tradition is one of extreme martial discipline and a fanatical devotion to the local gods of their rocky homeland. This clan represents the raw and untamed energy of the Rajput spirit providing the union with its most aggressive and fearless fighting units. During the imperial collapse they have carved out a reputation as the most dangerous individual combatants in the subcontinent feared by both Mughal and Maratha alike. Their leadership is defined by a direct and honest approach to war where the only acceptable outcome is victory or a glorious death on the field. They are the unbreakable storm of the Rajput union.

The guardians of the deep Thar desert who control the strategic caravan routes to the western world and possess an unparalleled knowledge of the sands. They are experts in camel warfare and long range scouting using the harsh environment of the desert as their primary ally against invaders. Their warriors are resilient and independent accustomed to surviving for weeks in the deep desert with minimal water and supplies. This clan represents the western frontier of the Rajput world securing the vital routes that connect the subcontinent with the Persian and Arab trade networks. In the civil war they serve as the primary scouts and frontier guards providing the union with early warning of any movement from the Afghan or Mughal west. Their influence is built on their control over the hidden wells and secret paths of the desert making them the indispensable masters of the Thar. They are the watchful and enduring lords of the desert sands.

A clan of highlanders from the northern reaches of Rajasthan who provide the union with its most professional and sought after mercenary infantry battalions. They are known for their adaptability and their willingness to embrace modern military technology including the latest European flintlocks and artillery. Their warriors are seasoned professionals who have fought in every corner of the subcontinent serving as the backbone of many regional armies. This clan represents the modernizing and entrepreneurial side of the Rajput world proving that tradition can be successfully blended with modern efficiency. During the current conflict they serve as the elite infantry of the union capable of holding the line against the most disciplined imperial forces. Their leadership is defined by a focus on tactical results and the maintenance of a well equipped and well paid standing army. They are the professional and formidable soldiers of fortune of the Rajput union.

Masters of artillery and fortification who have turned their city of Kota into an impregnable bastion and the primary manufacturing center for Rajput weaponry. They are renowned for their technical skills in casting large cannons and the engineering of complex defensive works that can withstand prolonged bombardments. Their warriors are specialized gunners and technicians who provide the union with its essential heavy firepower during siege operations. This clan represents the technical and industrial strength of the Rajput people ensuring that they can compete in the new age of gunpowder warfare. During the imperial decline they have taken over the role of the primary arsenal for the union providing a steady supply of high quality arms and ammunition to the other clans. Their influence is built on their indispensable technical expertise and their control over the vital trade in iron and saltpeter. They are the innovative and powerful smiths of the Rajput world.

Tactical geniuses and administrative masters who often serve as the regents and military advisors for the other great Rajput houses during times of crisis. They are known for their ability to organize complex logistics and their skill in navigating the treacherous waters of inter clan diplomacy. Their power is not based on large territories but on their indispensable intellectual and strategic contributions to the union's collective defense. This clan represents the strategic brains behind the Rajput resistance ensuring that the bravery of the other clans is always used to the greatest effect. In the context of the civil war they act as the primary coordinators of the union's forces ensuring that the disparate clan armies can operate as a single unified machine. Their leadership is defined by a cool and analytical approach to power often securing victory through clever maneuvering rather than raw force. They are the brilliant and necessary strategists of the Rajput union.

An ancient and storied lineage that guards the sacred hills near Delhi and maintains the northern border of the Rajput world against the constant pressure of the capital. They are specialists in defensive warfare and the conduct of raids into the imperial heartland using their proximity to the capital to gather vital intelligence. Their warriors are veteran fighters who have spent centuries in the shadow of the Mughals mastering the art of the border skirmish. This clan represents the northern outpost of the Rajput spirit serving as the first line of defense against any imperial attempt to reclaim Rajasthan. During the civil war they act as the primary eyes and ears of the union in the north providing early warning of every shift in the politics of the Delhi court. Their loyalty is to the preservation of the Rajput frontier and they have become the most seasoned border lords of the union. They are the watchful and resilient guardians of the northern hills.

Hill fort specialists who control the narrow passes and rocky defiles of the Aravalli mountains and extract heavy tolls from any army that dares to enter their territory. They are experts in mountain warfare and the construction of hidden defenses that can trap and decimate a much larger force within the rocky gaps. Their warriors are hardy and independent accustomed to the difficult life of the high crags and the constant vigilance required to protect their mountain homes. This clan represents the rugged and untameable spirit of the central highlands providing the union with its most secure interior bastions. In the civil war they serve as the primary defensive force of the mountain heartland ensuring that no invader can ever truly conquer the core of Rajputana. Their influence is built on their control over the vital mountain passes making them the gatekeepers of the interior trade and communication routes. They are the fierce and independent lords of the high crags.

Eastern warriors who act as the mobile vanguard for the union during its frequent campaigns into the fertile plains of central India and beyond. They are known for their skill in both heavy cavalry charges and the conduct of rapid infantry maneuvers across diverse terrains. Their military tradition is one of constant expansion and the defense of the eastern borders against the rising power of the Marathas and the Mughals. This clan represents the outward looking and aggressive side of the Rajput union seeking to project power far beyond the traditional borders of Rajasthan. During the current conflict they serve as the primary scouts and vanguards who lead the union's armies into the rich lands of the east. Their leadership is defined by a focus on mobility and the acquisition of new territories to strengthen the union's economic base. They are the daring and expansive warriors of the eastern frontier.

The hereditary prime ministers who serve as the actual executive heads of the Maratha confederacy and have directed its expansion across the entire subcontinent. From their seat in Pune they manage a sprawling network of generals and governors who have brought the Mughal empire to its knees. Their military strategy emphasizes extreme mobility and the systematic destruction of enemy supply lines through the use of light cavalry. They are the primary architects of the new Indian order that seeks to replace the Timurid hegemony with a Maratha led confederation. This ruling clan has transformed the Maratha movement from a local mountain resistance into a sophisticated imperial state with its own complex bureaucracy and revenue systems. Their court is a center of intense political activity where the grand strategy for the conquest of India is debated and decided. They represent the administrative and strategic genius of the Maratha people coordinating the efforts of the diverse clans toward a single common goal.

The royal house of the Chhatrapati which remains the ultimate symbol of Maratha pride and the foundational lineage that gave birth to the entire empire. While the executive power has shifted to the ministers they still maintain a sacred authority that is recognized by every Maratha warrior and peasant. Their influence is spiritual and cultural serving as the moral center around which the entire confederacy unites in times of existential threat. This clan represents the legacy of Shivaji Maharaj and the ideal of Hindavi Swarajya reminding the people of their roots and their divine mission. In the context of the civil war they act as the ultimate arbiters of legitimacy ensuring that the various generals and ministers remain loyal to the core principles of the Maratha state. Their presence in the royal capital of Satara provides a sense of continuity and stability that is essential for the cohesion of the sprawling empire. They are the sacred and honored guardians of the Maratha soul.

A powerful military clan based in the north that has taken on the responsibility of guarding the frontiers of the empire near the imperial capital and the Afghan border. They are unique among the Marathas for their heavy investment in modern European style infantry and a formidable artillery corps that can match any global power. Their forces act as the primary check against Afghan invasions and are the main force involved in the direct and dangerous politics of the Delhi court. This clan represents the transformation of the Maratha movement into a professionalized and modern imperial military power. During the current conflict they serve as the heavy muscle of the confederacy capable of holding ground in the plains and engaging in protracted siege warfare. Their leadership is defined by a focus on military innovation and the creation of a standing army that can operate far from the traditional Deccan heartland. They are the iron fist of the Maratha expansion in the north.

Masters of the Ganimi Kava guerrilla tactics who are famous for their lightning fast cavalry raids and their populist approach to leadership and warfare. They are known for their ability to traverse vast distances with incredible speed striking at the heart of enemy territory before a defense can even be organized. Their warriors are primarily light cavalry who pride themselves on their mobility and their deep connection to the common people of the Maratha lands. This clan represents the populist and revolutionary spirit of the Maratha movement prioritizing results and merit over pedigree and ritual. In the civil war they serve as the primary raiding force that devastates enemy economies and forces the Mughals to pay tribute. Their leadership is characterized by a fierce independence and a commitment to the welfare of their soldiers making them one of the most beloved factions in the empire. They are the swift and daring storm of the Maratha confederacy.

Wealthy lords of the western coast who control the rich textile trade of Gujarat and provide the empire with the massive financial resources needed to fund its endless campaigns. They govern a prosperous and stable province that acts as the primary economic engine of the Maratha state ensuring a steady flow of silver to the central treasury. Their military force is a mix of traditional cavalry and specialized coastal infantry who protect the vital ports and trade routes of the west. This clan represents the commercial and economic strength of the Maratha empire proving that trade is as important as the sword in the building of a great power. During the imperial decline they have expanded their influence into the northern plains of the west providing the empire with a secure base for operations into the heart of the Mughal lands. Their influence is built on their control over the lucrative maritime trade making them one of the most powerful and stable factions in the confederacy. They are the wealthy and efficient merchant kings of the Maratha world.

An independent minded branch of the royal house that has expanded Maratha influence into the eastern forests and the rich lands of the Bengal frontier. They are known for their fierce autonomy and their willingness to chart their own course regardless of the directives from the central government in Pune. Their military specialty is in forest warfare and the conduct of deep raids into the eastern provinces using the rugged terrain of the central hills as their base. This clan represents the eastern expansion of the Maratha spirit seeking to create a new and independent power base in the heart of the subcontinent. In the current conflict they act as a separate power block that often challenges the central authority while remaining committed to the overall success of the Maratha cause. Their leadership is defined by a focus on territorial expansion and the acquisition of new tribute from the wealthy subahs of the east. They are the ambitious and independent explorers of the Maratha world.

Disciplined and scholarly warriors who provide the moral leadership and the tactical order for the confederacy and are known for their deep religious devotion and martial skill. They represent the intellectual and traditionalist wing of the Maratha nobility ensuring that the values of the Brahmin warrior class are maintained in the army. Their military retinues are small but incredibly well disciplined and well equipped serving as the elite heavy cavalry during major field battles. This clan is dedicated to the preservation of the religious and social order of the Maratha state and they are the primary opponents of any form of disorder or lack of discipline. During the civil war they provide the state with its most reliable and principled officers who are capable of maintaining the morale of the diverse Maratha forces. Their loyalty is to the concept of the virtuous state and they are the primary guardians of the Maratha ethical code. They are the scholarly and formidable protectors of the Maratha social order.

The legendary admirals of the Maratha navy who protect the western coast from European fleets and local pirates and maintain a string of impregnable sea forts. They are the masters of the Konkan coast using their specialized knowledge of the tides and the rocky inlets to outmaneuver much larger and more modern naval powers. Their warriors are expert sailors and marines who bring a unique and essential maritime dimension to the Maratha military capabilities. This clan represents the naval strength of the empire ensuring that the coastal trade routes remain open and that the western borders are secure from sea-borne invasion. In the context of the civil war they serve as the primary guardians of the coast providing the empire with a secure maritime base and a steady supply of resources from the sea. Their influence is built on their control over the vital coastal ports making them the indispensable sea lords of the Maratha world. They are the daring and unbreakable defenders of the western waters.
